title: coix gigantea subsp. aquatica
intro: Coix gigantea subsp. aquatica, also known as water jobstears or aquatic Job's tears, is a subspecies of the Coix gigantea plant, which is a type of grass native to tropical Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of starch and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Coix gigantea subsp. aquatica include its ability to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine.
